item.title: A message from Mme Perron:
item.description: A friendly reminder that anyone interested in purchasing a Charcuterie Board from the Europe Team for the Easter Long Weekend that orders are due in by this Friday, March 6th. Please see Mme Perron or another teacher supervisor to place your orders.

item.title: A message from Ms. Clouthier:
item.description: This is a reminder that there will be a 7&8 Boys Basketball practice tomorrow after school from 2:15 until 3:45pm. Please make sure you give Ms. Clouthier your signed permission form by morning announcements tomorrow in order for you to play on Wednesday.

item.title: A message from: Mrs. Longval.
item.description: We have our final try-out today afterschool from 2:20-3:30pm for BOTH JR and SR Badminton players.

item.title: A message from Mrs. Longval:
item.description: Weight Room is OPEN this week at lunch on Monday - Wednesday and Friday and Thursday for Female students. There is NO open Weight room afterschool on Tuesday - due to the CrossFit Team Practice.

item.title: A message from Mr. Benoit:
item.description: It was an exciting day for hockey in Finch on Friday! The Ravens Tier 1 team put in an incredible effort, competing in five games in a single day and battling their way to the finals. They came up just short in a tight 1-0 loss to a strong St. Joe's team from Cornwall. The players worked hard throughout the day and demonstrated outstanding sportsmanship in every game. Mr. Benoit and Mr. McLaren were extremely proud of the team's performance. A shout out to Emery and Hudson as well for their support and help!

item.title: A message from Mme. Perron:
item.description: Could all Europe travelers please return all 50/50 tickets money collected and unsold tickets to Mme Perron as soon as possible. Leftover tickets are needed. Please return to room 125.

item.title: A message from Mr. Vonesch:
item.description: As you know, each year during Lent we always do a fundraiser for an international project, and this year we are raising money in support of recovery efforts in Jamaica! Hurricane Melissa swept across the Caribbean in late 2025, leaving widespread destruction in Jamaica, and these massive weather events always affect the poor more than anyone else. Our fundraising efforts will go towards emergency shelter and assistance, safe water and hygiene, home repair and recovery, and livelihood restoration through Catholic Relief Services. Together, let’s make a difference!

item.title: A message from Mr. D'Alessio:
item.description: There is a Debate Team Meeting tomorrow at 10:35 in Rm.209
